levels.name: Physical Process
levels.description: The actual physical processes, sensors, actuators, and field instruments that monitor and control industrial operations. This level represents the physical plant — pumps, valves, motors, boilers, turbines, and the sensors that measure their state.

levels.name: Intelligent Field Devices
levels.description: Programmable Logic Controllers (PLCs), Remote Terminal Units (RTUs), Intelligent Electronic Devices (IEDs), and other programmable field controllers that execute local control logic and interface directly with Level 0 physical process equipment.

levels.name: Control Systems
levels.description: SCADA systems, Distributed Control Systems (DCS), Human-Machine Interfaces (HMIs), and supervisory control applications that monitor and control Level 1 field devices. This is the primary control network where operators interact with the physical process.

levels.name: Operations / Manufacturing Operations
levels.description: Manufacturing operations management systems including process historians, manufacturing execution systems (MES), laboratory information management systems (LIMS), and operational reporting systems. Bridges between plant-floor control and enterprise IT.

levels.name: Enterprise / Business Network
levels.description: Enterprise IT systems including business intelligence, ERP, corporate email, file shares, and internet connectivity. This zone bridges industrial operations with corporate business functions and represents the primary entry point for IT-origin threats that pivot into OT environments.

zones.name: Safety Instrumented System Zone
zones.description: Logically and physically separated SIS network providing last-line-of-defense safety functions. Should be isolated from all other networks.

zones.name: Process Control Network
zones.description: The primary OT control network connecting field devices (L1) with supervisory control systems (L2). Typically uses industrial protocols: Modbus, DNP3, PROFINET, EtherNet/IP.

zones.name: IT/OT DMZ
zones.description: The demilitarized zone providing a controlled interface between the Operations network (L3) and the Enterprise network (L4). Critical segmentation boundary for IT/OT convergence security.
